---
title: "Conclusions"
filters:
  - shinylive
format:
  reefcode-html:
    embed-resources: false
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
suppressWarnings(suppressPackageStartupMessages({library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(patchwork); library(kableExtra)}))
pre <- readRDS("../data/app/precomputed.rds")
palette_grp <- c("Common.Broadcaster" = "#0072B2", "Rare.Broadcaster" = "#56B4E9",
                 "Common.Brooder" = "#D55E00", "Rare.Brooder" = "#E69F00")
label_grp <- c("Common.Broadcaster" = "Common broadcaster", "Rare.Broadcaster" = "Rare broadcaster",
               "Common.Brooder" = "Common brooder", "Rare.Brooder" = "Rare brooder")

# Load the published analysis objects for the highlight-result figures below. This page renders
# after the analysis pages, so their timestamped RData is already on disk.
source("../_includes/_analysis_helpers.R")
repo_root <- find_repo_root()
asp <- setNames(lapply(c("TCRMP", "VINPS", "CSUN"), function(p)
  tryCatch(load_latest_program(p, "aspublished", dir = file.path(repo_root, "data", "rdata")),
           error = function(e) NULL)), c("TCRMP", "VINPS", "CSUN"))
have_asp <- all(!sapply(asp, is.null))
source("../_includes/_brooder_proportion.R")
brooder <- tryCatch(compute_brooder_series(repo_root), error = function(e) NULL)
program_colors <- c(CSUN = "#009E73", TCRMP = "#0072B2", VINPS = "#D55E00")
```

> Olinger LK, Edmunds PJ, Levitan D, Smith TB, Lasker H, Feeley M, Mahoney L, Dahl A. Reproductive mode, but not rarity, influences population trajectories in corals. In Review 2026.

This page gathers the headline results of the study and pairs them with a tool to test the one choice they rest on. The figures below show the published analysis; open any program page for the full analysis and its updated version.

## Highlight results

### The integrated result

The headline result combines the three programs in one figure (@fig-main-plot). Each program contributes a row: on the left, the baseline abundance distribution with every taxon colored by its group; on the right, the site-level group cover over time on a log scale, with a linear trend per group. Read across the three rows, broadcast-spawning corals decline steeply while brooding corals hold or rise, and within each reproductive mode the common and rare taxa fall at the same proportional rate. The pattern tracks reproductive mode, not rarity.

```{r fig-main-plot}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 12
#| fig-cap: "Integrated abundance and trend figure across the three stony-coral programs (as published). Rows: TCRMP (A, B), VINPS (C, D), CSUN (E, F). Left panels rank baseline taxa by mean cover and color them by group; right panels show site-level group cover over time on a log scale with a per-group linear fit."
if (have_asp) {
  build_panel <- function(e, tcol) {
    b <- e$baseline; b <- b[b$meancov > 0, ]
    b$grp <- interaction(b$commonness, b$Reproductive_mode)
    b[[tcol]] <- factor(b[[tcol]], levels = rev(b[[tcol]][order(b$rank)]))
    p_bar <- ggplot(b, aes(.data[[tcol]], meancov, fill = grp)) +
      geom_col(width = 0.8) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = palette_grp, labels = label_grp, name = NULL, drop = FALSE) +
      coord_flip() + labs(x = NULL, y = "Baseline percent cover") +
      theme_classic(base_size = 9) +
      theme(legend.position = "none", axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 6))
    ad <- e$analysis_data; ad$grp <- interaction(ad$category, ad$Reproductive_mode)
    p_tr <- ggplot(ad, aes(year, perccover, color = grp, fill = grp)) +
      geom_jitter(shape = 21, color = "black", alpha = 0.12, width = 0.25, height = 0, show.legend = FALSE) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 0.7) +
      scale_color_manual(values = palette_grp, labels = label_grp, name = NULL, drop = FALSE) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = palette_grp, labels = label_grp, name = NULL, drop = FALSE) +
      scale_y_log10() + labs(x = "Year", y = "Percent cover (log scale)") +
      theme_classic(base_size = 9) + theme(legend.position = "none")
    p_bar | p_tr
  }
  (build_panel(asp$TCRMP, "coralSpecies")) /
    (build_panel(asp$VINPS, "coralSpecies")) /
    (build_panel(asp$CSUN,  "coralGenera")) +
    plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A") &
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
}
```

The model behind the figure is a linear model of log₁₀ cover on year, rarity, reproductive mode, and every interaction, fit separately for each program with HC3 robust standard errors [@Zeileis2004]. @tbl-main-stats reports the coefficients side by side. The three-way Year × Rarity × Reproductive-mode term is where a rarity effect that differs by reproductive mode would appear.

```{r tbl-main-stats}
#| echo: false
if (have_asp) {
  term_labels_t1 <- c(
    "(Intercept)" = "Intercept", "yearind" = "Year", "categoryRare" = "Rarity",
    "Reproductive_modeBrooder" = "Repro. mode (brooder)", "yearind:categoryRare" = "Year x Rarity",
    "yearind:Reproductive_modeBrooder" = "Year x Repro. mode",
    "categoryRare:Reproductive_modeBrooder" = "Rarity x Repro. mode",
    "yearind:categoryRare:Reproductive_modeBrooder" = "Year x Rarity x Repro. mode")
  terms_t1 <- names(term_labels_t1)
  mk_col <- function(e) {
    cr <- e$coef_robust
    v <- sprintf("%.3f (%.3f)%s", cr$estimate, cr$std.error,
                 ifelse(cr$p.value < 0.001, " ***", ifelse(cr$p.value < 0.01, " **",
                        ifelse(cr$p.value < 0.05, " *", ""))))
    setNames(v, cr$term)[terms_t1]
  }
  t1 <- data.frame(Predictor = term_labels_t1[terms_t1],
                   TCRMP = mk_col(asp$TCRMP), VINPS = mk_col(asp$VINPS), CSUN = mk_col(asp$CSUN),
                   row.names = NULL, check.names = FALSE)
  kbl(t1, align = c("l", "r", "r", "r"),
      caption = "Table 1. Integrated model coefficients (estimate, HC3 robust SE) across the three programs, as published. Stars mark significance: *** p < 0.001, ** p < 0.01, * p < 0.05. Labels read Rarity.") |>
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
}
```

### Occupancy versus extent

Two taxa can lose the same amount of cover in different ways: one by disappearing from sites (an occupancy loss), another by thinning where it persists (an extent loss). @fig-arrows-plot separates the two. Each arrow runs from a taxon's baseline occupancy and extent to its recent values, colored by group. Broadcasters tend to slide down and to the left (thinner and less widespread); several brooders hold or expand their occupancy.

```{r fig-arrows-plot}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 4.5
#| fig-cap: "Occupancy (x) versus extent (y), baseline to recent, per program (A TCRMP, B VINPS, C CSUN), as published. Occupancy is the proportion of surveyed sites where the taxon was present; extent is the mean cover where present. Both axes are log scaled."
if (have_asp) {
  arrows_panel <- function(e, prog) {
    st <- e$species_table
    st <- st[is.finite(st$occ_start) & is.finite(st$occ_end) &
             is.finite(st$ext_start) & is.finite(st$ext_end) &
             st$occ_start > 0 & st$occ_end > 0 & st$ext_start > 0 & st$ext_end > 0, ]
    st$grp <- interaction(st$commonness, st$mode)
    ggplot(st, aes(occ_start / 100, ext_start, color = grp)) +
      geom_segment(aes(xend = occ_end / 100, yend = ext_end),
                   arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.15, "cm"), type = "closed"), linewidth = 0.4) +
      geom_point(size = 1.1) +
      scale_color_manual(values = palette_grp, labels = label_grp, name = NULL, drop = FALSE) +
      scale_x_log10() + scale_y_log10() +
      labs(x = "Occupancy (proportion of sites)", y = "Extent (cover where present, %)", title = prog) +
      theme_classic(base_size = 9) + theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  }
  (arrows_panel(asp$TCRMP, "TCRMP") | arrows_panel(asp$VINPS, "VINPS") | arrows_panel(asp$CSUN, "CSUN")) +
    plot_layout(guides = "collect") + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A") &
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
}
```

### Brooders and broadcasters over time

Reproductive mode reshapes the community. @fig-brooder-broadcaster-cover follows the mean cover of broadcasters and brooders separately in each program, and tracks the brooder share of total coral cover. Broadcaster cover falls across all three programs while brooder cover holds steadier, so the brooder share of cover climbs over the record. In two programs it approaches the 50 percent parity line, meaning brooders may soon hold as much reef cover as broadcasters, a reversal of the historical dominance of broadcasting reef-builders.

```{r fig-brooder-broadcaster-cover}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-cap: "Brooder versus broadcaster cover over time, all taxa (as published). (A) Mean cover per site for broadcasters (solid) and brooders (dashed) in each program. (B) Brooder proportion of total coral cover, with the dashed line marking 50 percent parity."
if (!is.null(brooder)) {
  cover_all <- brooder$cover_all
  ratio_all <- brooder$ratio
  p_cover <- ggplot(cover_all, aes(year, mean_cover, color = program, linetype = Reproductive_mode)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) + geom_point(size = 1.1, alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_color_manual(values = program_colors, name = "Program") +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = c(Broadcaster = "solid", Brooder = "dashed"), name = "Reproductive mode") +
    labs(x = "Year", y = "Mean cover per site (%)") + theme_classic(base_size = 11)
  p_prop <- ggplot(ratio_all, aes(year, prop_brooder, color = program)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) + geom_point(size = 1.3) +
    scale_color_manual(values = program_colors, name = "Program") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(0, 0.65)) +
    labs(x = "Year", y = "Brooder proportion of coral cover") + theme_classic(base_size = 11)
  (p_cover + p_prop) + plot_layout(widths = c(1.2, 1)) + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
}
```

## Test the rarity boundary

The rarity result rests on one choice: the boundary that separates common taxa from rare taxa along the ranked abundance distribution. The manuscript sets it where the top taxa reach 90 percent of baseline cover. This tool exposes that choice. At the paper's boundary the tool reproduces the published results.

## The live tool (runs in your browser)

The app below runs entirely in your browser through shinylive (WebAssembly R). The first load fetches the R runtime, so give it a moment. Every partition is pre-solved, so moving a boundary swaps stored results instead of fitting a model.

```{shinylive-r}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 820
## file: app.R
# webR-safe (shinylive) version of the rare/common boundary explorer.
# Uses only base R + shiny + dplyr (no ggplot2, no flextable): ggplot2's dependency chain
# (munsell/scales/...) does not resolve in webR, and flextable/officer do not run there.
# The plots are drawn in base graphics and Table 1 is hand-built HTML. Every partition is
# pre-solved (bundled in pre_b64.txt), so nothing is fitted at run time.
library(shiny)
library(dplyr)

b64 <- paste(readLines("pre_b64.txt"), collapse = "")
pre <- unserialize(memDecompress(jsonlite::base64_dec(b64), "gzip"))
progs <- c("TCRMP", "VINPS", "CSUN")

palette_grp <- c("Common.Broadcaster" = "#0072B2", "Rare.Broadcaster" = "#56B4E9",
                 "Common.Brooder" = "#D55E00", "Rare.Brooder" = "#E69F00")
label_grp <- c("Common.Broadcaster" = "Common broadcaster", "Rare.Broadcaster" = "Rare broadcaster",
               "Common.Brooder" = "Common brooder", "Rare.Brooder" = "Rare brooder")
term_labels <- c("(Intercept)" = "Intercept", "yearind" = "Year",
  "categoryRare" = "Category (Rare)", "Reproductive_modeBrooder" = "Repro. mode (Brooder)",
  "yearind:categoryRare" = "Year x Category", "yearind:Reproductive_modeBrooder" = "Year x Repro. mode",
  "categoryRare:Reproductive_modeBrooder" = "Category x Repro. mode",
  "yearind:categoryRare:Reproductive_modeBrooder" = "Year x Cat x Repro")

# --- base-R plots -----------------------------------------------------------
plot_sad <- function(prog, part) {
  d <- part$classification
  ord <- order(prog$baseline$rank[match(d$taxon, prog$baseline$taxon)])
  d <- d[ord, ]                                   # most abundant first
  grp <- paste(d$commonness, d$Reproductive_mode, sep = ".")
  cols <- palette_grp[grp]; cols[is.na(cols)] <- "grey70"
  op <- par(mar = c(4, 11, 3, 1)); on.exit(par(op))
  bp <- barplot(rev(d$meancov), horiz = TRUE, col = rev(cols), border = NA,
                names.arg = rev(d$taxon), las = 1, cex.names = 0.72,
                xlab = "Mean baseline abundance",
                main = sprintf("%d common / %d rare", part$n_common, prog$N - part$n_common))
  nb <- nrow(d)
  if (part$n_common >= 1 && part$n_common < nb)   # boundary between common and rare bars
    abline(h = mean(bp[c(nb - part$n_common, nb - part$n_common + 1)]), lty = 2, lwd = 2, col = "grey10")
}

plot_trend <- function(part) {
  d <- part$analysis
  grp <- paste(d$category, d$Reproductive_mode, sep = ".")
  cols <- palette_grp[grp]
  op <- par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 1, 1)); on.exit(par(op))
  plot(d$year, d$perccover, log = "y", pch = 21, cex = 1.1,
       bg = adjustcolor(cols, 0.35), col = "#00000055",
       xlab = "Year", ylab = "Abundance (log scale)")
  for (g in unique(grp)) {
    sub <- d[grp == g, ]
    if (nrow(sub) > 2) {
      m <- lm(log10(perccover) ~ year, sub)
      xs <- range(sub$year); ys <- 10^predict(m, data.frame(year = xs))
      lines(xs, ys, col = palette_grp[g], lwd = 2.5)
    }
  }
  legend("topright", legend = label_grp[names(palette_grp)], col = palette_grp,
         lwd = 2.5, cex = 0.85, bty = "n")
}

part_of <- function(p, k) {
  pp <- pre[[p]]
  key <- as.character(min(max(round(k), min(pp$valid_k)), max(pp$valid_k)))
  pp$parts[[key]]
}

ui <- fluidPage(
  tags$style(HTML(
    "body{font-family:'Space Grotesk',system-ui,sans-serif}
     .pubtbl{border-collapse:collapse;font-size:13px;margin:6px 0 4px}
     .pubtbl th,.pubtbl td{border-bottom:1px solid #ddd6ca;padding:5px 12px;text-align:center;vertical-align:top}
     .pubtbl td:first-child,.pubtbl th:first-child{text-align:left}
     .pubtbl thead th{background:#0b2444;color:#fff;white-space:pre-line;font-weight:600}")),
  h4("Rare / common boundary explorer"),
  p("Move each program's boundary. Taxa above the split are common, below are rare. The abundance ",
    "distribution, the temporal trends, and Table 1 all rebuild together. Each slider starts at the ",
    "paper's 0.90 boundary."),
  actionButton("reset", "Reset all to the paper's boundary (0.90)"),
  tags$hr(),
  lapply(progs, function(p) tagList(
    h5(sprintf("%s  (%s)", p, pre[[p]]$taxon_level)),
    uiOutput(paste0("sl_", p)),
    fluidRow(column(5, plotOutput(paste0("sad_", p), height = "400px")),
             column(7, plotOutput(paste0("trend_", p), height = "400px"))),
    tags$hr()
  )),
  h5("Table 1, regenerated at the current boundaries"),
  uiOutput("table1"),
  br(),
  h5("Supplemental: group-specific temporal slopes"),
  tableOutput("slopes")
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  po <- function(p) part_of(p, input[[paste0("k_", p)]])
  lapply(progs, function(p) {
    output[[paste0("sl_", p)]] <- renderUI({
      pp <- pre[[p]]
      sliderInput(paste0("k_", p), sprintf("common taxa (%s)", p),
                  min = min(pp$valid_k), max = max(pp$valid_k), value = pp$k_paper, step = 1, width = "100%")
    })
    output[[paste0("sad_", p)]]   <- renderPlot({ req(input[[paste0("k_", p)]]); plot_sad(pre[[p]], po(p)) })
    output[[paste0("trend_", p)]] <- renderPlot({ req(input[[paste0("k_", p)]]); plot_trend(po(p)) })
  })
  observeEvent(input$reset, { for (p in progs) updateSliderInput(session, paste0("k_", p), value = pre[[p]]$k_paper) })

  output$table1 <- renderUI({
    req(input$k_TCRMP, input$k_VINPS, input$k_CSUN)
    parts <- lapply(progs, po); req(all(!vapply(parts, is.null, logical(1))))
    f2 <- function(x) formatC(x, format = "f", digits = 2)
    cell <- function(est, se, p) {
      st <- ifelse(p < 0.001, "***", ifelse(p < 0.01, "**", ifelse(p < 0.05, "*", "")))
      pt <- ifelse(p < 0.001, "p<0.001", paste0("p=", formatC(p, format = "f", digits = 3)))
      HTML(paste0(f2(est), " (", f2(se), ")<br>", pt, st))
    }
    co1 <- parts[[1]]$coef
    body <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(co1)), function(i)
      tags$tr(tags$td(term_labels[[co1$term[i]]]),
        lapply(seq_along(progs), function(j) {
          co <- parts[[j]]$coef; sig <- co$p.value[i] < 0.05
          tags$td(style = if (sig) "font-weight:700" else "", cell(co$estimate[i], co$std.error[i], co$p.value[i]))
        })))
    stat_labs <- c("Adj. R-squared", "Observations", "Residual SE", "df")
    stat_vals <- lapply(parts, function(pt) { fs <- pt$fit_stats
      c(formatC(fs$adj_r2, format = "f", digits = 3), fs$n, formatC(fs$rse, format = "f", digits = 3), fs$df) })
    stats <- lapply(seq_along(stat_labs), function(r)
      tags$tr(tags$td(stat_labs[r]), lapply(stat_vals, function(v) tags$td(v[r]))))
    tags$div(
      tags$table(class = "pubtbl",
        tags$thead(tags$tr(tags$th("Predictor"),
          lapply(progs, function(p) tags$th(paste0(p, "\nEstimate (SE)\nP-value"))))),
        tags$tbody(body, stats)),
      tags$p(style = "font-size:12px;color:#6d685e",
             "Table 1. Linear-model coefficients with HC3 robust standard errors. Dependent variable: ",
             "log10 percent cover. Significant terms (p<0.05) in bold."))
  })

  output$slopes <- renderTable({
    req(input$k_TCRMP, input$k_VINPS, input$k_CSUN)
    do.call(rbind, lapply(progs, function(p) {
      po(p)$slopes %>% transmute(Program = p, Group,
        Slope = sprintf("%.4f", Slope), SE = sprintf("%.4f", SE),
        `Annual % change` = sprintf("%.2f", `Annual % change`),
        `P-value` = ifelse(`P-value` < 0.001, "< 0.001", sprintf("%.3f", `P-value`)))
    }))
  }, striped = TRUE, hover = TRUE, width = "100%")
}
shinyApp(ui, server)
```

## What moving the boundary does

The comparison below shows the effect without the app. For TCRMP, it moves the boundary to three positions and reports the group slopes.

```{r demo-sweep}
#| code-summary: "Show code: TCRMP group slopes at three boundary positions"
ks <- c(5, pre$TCRMP$k_paper, 20)
do.call(rbind, lapply(ks, function(k) {
  pt <- pre$TCRMP$parts[[as.character(k)]]
  pt$slopes |>
    transmute(`Common taxa` = k, `Cumulative cover` = sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * pt$cum_cover),
              Group, `Annual % change` = sprintf("%.2f", `Annual % change`),
              `P-value` = ifelse(`P-value` < 0.001, "< 0.001", sprintf("%.3f", `P-value`)))
})) |>
  kbl(align = c("r", "r", "l", "r", "r"),
      caption = "TCRMP group slopes at three boundaries. The middle block is the paper's 0.90 boundary.") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1:2, valign = "top")
```

The classification changes, and the slopes move with it, but the ranking of the four groups by rate of decline stays stable.

## Download the data and code

The complete input data for every program (scoped to the as-published window), all derived datasets, all of the metadata, and the full analysis code are available for download here.

Analysis code:

- TCRMP: {{< downloadthis ../_includes/_analysis_tcrmp.qmd dname="analysis_tcrmp.qmd" label="TCRMP code (.qmd)" >}}
- VINPS: {{< downloadthis ../_includes/_analysis_vinps.qmd dname="analysis_vinps.qmd" label="VINPS code (.qmd)" >}}
- CSUN: {{< downloadthis ../_includes/_analysis_csun.qmd dname="analysis_csun.qmd" label="CSUN code (.qmd)" >}}
- Octocorals: {{< downloadthis ../_includes/_analysis_octo.qmd dname="analysis_octo.qmd" label="Octocoral code (.qmd)" >}}

Data and metadata:

```{r downloads-all}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
.files <- sort(list.files("../data/derived", pattern = "^pub_rarity_.*\\.(csv|txt)$"))
if (length(.files)) {
  cat("| File | Type | Download |\n|:--|:--|:--|\n")
  for (f in .files) {
    lbl <- sub("^pub_rarity_", "", f)
    typ <- if (grepl("\\.txt$", f)) "metadata" else "data"
    cat(sprintf("| `%s` | %s | [download](../data/derived/%s){download=\"%s\"} |\n", lbl, typ, f, f))
  }
}
```
